_instructions: This is the JSON format Claude produces at the end of every audit session. Save as [client]-audit-data-[month]-[year].json in the project root. Then run: node scripts/fill-template.mjs [filename].json

scores._note: Count of each badge across ALL checks — standard 28 + all B-checks. Must add up to totalChecks.

execSummary.headline: One strong sentence summarizing the audit — e.g. 'Acme Plumbing has strong fundamentals but several technical gaps are actively suppressing search visibility.'
execSummary.body: Two to four sentences. What was strong, what needs work, what the client will have after the remedy.

deliverables._note: One entry per standalone code block included in the report. Empty array if none.
deliverables.desc: LocalBusiness schema JSON-LD — ready to paste into your site's code injection field
llms.txt — complete file written for this business, ready to publish at /llms.txt

deliverables._note: Example of a FAQPage schema deliverable — include when B_AI2 is a finding
deliverables.desc: FAQPage schema JSON-LD — structured Q&A ready to paste into code injection alongside the entity graph schema

priorityItems._note: All Criticals first, then High Values. No required count — use however many the findings warrant.
priorityItems.text: Plain-English title of this priority item, as it will appear in the Priority Action List.

remedyItems._note: Must be in the same order as priorityItems. One entry per priority item. When a remedy item covers multiple checks (i.e., two or more audit checks share the same remedyItem number), fill-template.mjs automatically renders a 'This remedy addresses N audit findings' disclosure note below the item title — no extra field needed. Write the findings text to describe all covered checks.
remedyItems.title: Full remedy item title (can be slightly longer than the priority list entry)
remedyItems.findings: One to three sentences describing exactly what was found on this client's site — specific and factual. No remedy steps here.
steps: Step 1: Exact platform path and action, written so a non-technical person can follow it.
Step 3: Add or remove steps — no required count.

remedyItems.title: Second remedy item title
remedyItems.findings: What was found for this check.

remedyItems._note: Example with a standalone code deliverable — full entity graph. Set standalone to null if not applicable.
remedyItems.title: Add Complete Schema Entity Graph
remedyItems.findings: No structured data markup was found on the homepage. Google, ChatGPT, and Perplexity cannot confirm your business name, address, phone number, or service area from machine-readable data. Without this, AI assistants cannot reliably recommend or describe your business when users ask about services in your area.
steps: Step 1: In Squarespace, go to Settings → Advanced → Code Injection.
Step 2: Paste the complete JSON-LD block below into the Header field.
Step 4: Test at search.google.com/test/rich-results to confirm Google can read the schema.
Step 5: Verify your Google Maps listing URL, Facebook page URL, and any other directory links in the sameAs array are correct before publishing.

standalone.label: Standalone Deliverable — Entity Graph Schema JSON-LD (WebSite + LocalBusiness + Person)
standalone.code: <script type="application/ld+json"> [ { "@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "WebSite", "@id": "https://acmeplumbing.com/#website", "url": "https://acmeplumbing.com", "name": "Acme Plumbing", "publisher": { "@id": "https://acmeplumbing.com/#organization" }, "potentialAction": { "@type": "SearchAction", "target": "https://acmeplumbing.com/?s={search_term_string}", "query-input": "required name=search_term_string" } }, { "@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "Plumber", "@id": "https://acmeplumbing.com/#organization", "name": "Acme Plumbing", "url": "https://acmeplumbing.com", "telephone": "+18175550100", "email": "info@acmeplumbing.com", "address": { "@type": "PostalAddress", "streetAddress": "123 Main St", "addressLocality": "Fort Worth", "addressRegion": "TX", "postalCode": "76102", "addressCountry": "US" }, "geo": { "@type": "GeoCoordinates", "latitude": 32.7555, "longitude": -97.3308 }, "hasMap": "https://maps.google.com/?cid=123456789", "openingHoursSpecification": [ { "@type": "OpeningHoursSpecification", "dayOfWeek": ["Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday"], "opens": "07:00", "closes": "17:00" } ], "priceRange": "$$", "knowsAbout": ["Residential Plumbing", "Water Heater Repair", "Drain Cleaning"], "sameAs": [ "https://maps.google.com/?cid=123456789", "https://www.facebook.com/acmeplumbing", "https://www.yelp.com/biz/acme-plumbing-fort-worth", "https://www.bbb.org/us/tx/fort-worth/profile/plumber/acme-plumbing" ] }, { "@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "@id": "https://acmeplumbing.com/#owner", "name": "John Smith", "worksFor": { "@id": "https://acmeplumbing.com/#organization" } } ] </script>

remedyItems._note: Example of an llms.txt remedy item. The code field contains the complete file content as plain text — no HTML spans needed, just the raw file text with \n for line breaks.
remedyItems.title: Publish an llms.txt File So AI Tools Know Who You Are
remedyItems.findings: No llms.txt file was found at acmeplumbing.com/llms.txt. AI assistants (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Siri) have no structured guidance on how to represent this business — they rely on general page content, which produces incomplete or inaccurate descriptions when users ask about local plumbers.
steps: Step 1: Copy the complete file text from the Standalone Deliverable below.
Step 2: In Squarespace, go to Pages → Not Linked → Add Blank Page. Title it 'llms' and set the URL slug to exactly 'llms.txt'.
Step 3: Add a Code Block to the page. Paste the file text into the code block.
Step 4: Publish the page, then verify it loads at acmeplumbing.com/llms.txt.

standalone.label: Standalone Deliverable — llms.txt (complete file, ready to publish)
standalone.code:
# Acme Plumbing
> Residential and commercial plumber serving Fort Worth, TX and surrounding Tarrant County communities.

Acme Plumbing is a licensed plumbing company serving homeowners and small businesses in Fort Worth and northern Tarrant County. We specialize in emergency repairs, water heater replacement, and drain cleaning, with same-day availability Monday through Friday.

## Services
- Emergency Plumbing Repair: Same-day service for burst pipes, leaks, and sewage backups. Call for pricing.
- Water Heater Replacement: Installation of gas, electric, and tankless water heaters. Most jobs completed in one visit.
- Drain Cleaning: Hydro-jetting and snake service for clogs in kitchen, bath, and main sewer lines.
- Fixture Installation: Faucets, toilets, garbage disposals, and shut-off valves.

## Contact
- Phone: (817) 555-0100
- Email: info@acmeplumbing.com
- Address: 123 Main St, Fort Worth, TX 76102
- Hours: Monday–Friday 7am–5pm. Emergency service available after hours.

## Frequently Asked Questions
Q: Do you offer emergency plumbing service?
A: Yes. We provide emergency service 7 days a week. Call (817) 555-0100 any time.

Q: What areas do you serve?
A: We serve Fort Worth and the surrounding communities in Tarrant County, including Arlington, Keller, Southlake, and Grapevine.

Q: Are you licensed and insured?
A: Yes. Acme Plumbing is fully licensed in Texas and carries general liability and workers' compensation insurance.

## About
Acme Plumbing was founded in 2008 by John Smith, a master plumber with 20 years of experience. We are a family-owned business committed to honest pricing and same-day service.

## Key Pages
- Homepage: https://acmeplumbing.com/
- Services: https://acmeplumbing.com/services
- About: https://acmeplumbing.com/about
- Contact: https://acmeplumbing.com/contact

remedyItems._note: Example of a FAQPage schema remedy item — only include when B_AI2 finds FAQ content on the site without FAQPage schema.
remedyItems.title: Add FAQPage Schema So AI Assistants Can Quote Your Answers Directly
remedyItems.findings: The site has a Frequently Asked Questions section, but no FAQPage structured data markup. Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, and Perplexity can surface FAQ answers directly in search results and AI responses — but only when those Q&A pairs are in FAQPage schema format.
steps: Step 1: In Squarespace, go to Settings → Advanced → Code Injection.
Step 2: Paste the FAQPage JSON-LD block below into the Header field, after the entity graph schema already added.
Step 3: Click Save, then test at search.google.com/test/rich-results — the tool should now show FAQ rich results as detected.

standalone.label: Standalone Deliverable — FAQPage Schema JSON-LD
standalone.code: <script type="application/ld+json"> { "@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "FAQPage", "mainEntity": [ { "@type": "Question", "name": "Do you offer emergency plumbing service?", "acceptedAnswer": { "@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. We provide emergency service 7 days a week. Call (817) 555-0100 any time." } }, { "@type": "Question", "name": "What areas do you serve?", "acceptedAnswer": { "@type": "Answer", "text": "We serve Fort Worth and surrounding communities in Tarrant County, including Arlington, Keller, Southlake, and Grapevine." } }, { "@type": "Question", "name": "Are you licensed and insured?", "acceptedAnswer": { "@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. Acme Plumbing is fully licensed in Texas and carries general liability and workers' compensation insurance." } } ] } </script>

auditChecks._note: All 28 standard checks required. Keys: C01_1 through C08_2. badgeClass: badge-critical | badge-high | badge-pass | badge-nice. badgeLabel: Critical | High Value | Pass | Nice to Have. remedyItem: integer (1-based) if this check is covered in Remedy Package, null otherwise. ⚠ RULE: Every check with badge-critical or badge-high MUST have a non-null remedyItem. Two checks may share the same remedyItem number only when their fix steps are literally identical. A null remedyItem on a Critical or High Value check is a production error.

C01_1.found: The page title tag is missing entirely.
C01_1.impact: Google has no text to display in search results for this page. It will either show the URL or auto-generate a title, usually poorly.

C01_2.found: Meta description is present but is 210 characters — Google truncates at ~155.
C01_2.impact: The search result snippet gets cut off mid-sentence, which reduces click-through rate.

C01_3.found: Canonical tag present and pointing to the correct URL.
C01_3.impact: Google knows which URL to index. No duplicate content risk.

C01_4.found: Robots meta tag set to index, follow — correct.
C01_4.impact: Search engines can crawl and index this page normally.

C01_5.found: Two H1 tags found on the page.
C01_5.impact: Google expects one H1 per page. Multiple H1s dilute the primary topic signal.

C01_6.found: Inner pages do not have canonical tags.
C01_6.impact: Low risk for a site this size, but worth adding as the site grows.

C02_1.found: OG image present at a reasonable size.
C02_1.impact: Social shares will show a proper preview image.

C02_2.found: og:title, og:description, og:url, og:type all present.
C02_2.impact: Social shares render correctly with correct title and description.

C02_3.found: Twitter card meta tags are absent.
C02_3.impact: X/Twitter will fall back to OG tags, which usually works. Not urgent.

C03_1.found: TTFB measured at 320ms via PageSpeed Insights.
C03_1.impact: Within the acceptable range. No action needed.

C03_2.found: Total load time 2.1s on desktop, 3.4s on mobile.
C03_2.impact: Acceptable. Mobile is slightly slow but not critical.

C03_3.found: Hero image is 2.4MB and served as PNG. No next-gen format in use.
C03_3.impact: This single image is adding ~1.8s to mobile load time.

C03_4.found: Only Squarespace's own scripts loading. No heavy third-party services detected.

C04_1.found: No structured data markup of any kind found on the homepage.
C04_1.impact: Google cannot confirm your business name, address, phone number, or hours from machine-readable data. No eligibility for rich results.

C04_2.found: N/A — no structured data present to evaluate connectivity.
C04_2.impact: Will be resolved when schema is added.

C04_3.found: LocalBusiness, BreadcrumbList, and FAQPage rich result types are all eligible but absent.
C04_3.impact: Missing out on enhanced search result features that competitors may already be using.

C05_1.found: GA4 property G-XXXXXXXXXX detected and firing correctly.
C05_1.impact: Analytics data is being collected. No action needed.

C05_2.found: GA4 tag fires once per page load.
C05_2.impact: No duplicate counting. Data is accurate.

C05_3.found: GA4, Squarespace analytics, and one font loader detected. No unexpected scripts.
C05_3.impact: Clean third-party inventory. No hidden performance or privacy exposure.

C06_1.found: robots.txt exists and allows all crawlers including GPTBot and ClaudeBot.
C06_1.impact: No accidental crawl blocks. AI search crawlers can index the site.

C06_2.impact: Secure connection. No browser warnings for visitors.

C06_3.found: Three external links found without rel="noopener" attribute.
C06_3.impact: Minor security exposure. Easy one-time fix.

C06_4.found: No llms.txt file found at acmeplumbing.com/llms.txt.
C06_4.impact: AI assistants (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Siri) have no structured guidance on how to represent this business. They may describe it inaccurately or incompletely.

C07_1.found: 7 of 12 images on the homepage are missing alt text.
C07_1.impact: Screen readers skip these images entirely. Google cannot index their content.

C07_2.impact: Screen readers and translation tools identify the language correctly.

C08_1.found: Squarespace — Squarespace handles hosting, CDN, and most technical SEO defaults automatically. Platform-specific instructions are provided throughout the Remedy Package.

C08_2._note: This check uses a 5-signal scoring rubric. See audit-session-guide.md Check 28 for full criteria. remedyItem should always be null — the individual failing checks (C04_1, C04_2, C06_4, B_AI2) carry their own remedy items.
C08_2.found: Score: 2/5. LocalBusiness schema: ✓ (present but minimal). sameAs links: ✗ (no directory links in schema). llms.txt: ✗ (404 — file missing). FAQPage schema: ✗ (no FAQ content on site). Static text content: ✓ (homepage has readable NAP and service descriptions).
C08_2.impact: Gaps in signals 2 and 3 are the highest priority. sameAs links are addressed in the Schema remedy; the llms.txt file is provided as a standalone deliverable in that same remedy.

bChecks._note: One entry per additional diagnostic found during the live session. id follows B01, B02 convention. Empty array if no B-checks.
bChecks.name: Mobile Viewport Meta Tag
bChecks.found: Viewport meta tag present and correctly configured.
bChecks.impact: Mobile browsers render the page at the correct scale.

bChecks.name: HTTP to HTTPS Redirect
bChecks.impact: No insecure entry points. Redirect chain is clean.
